revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/siroisiinariyamakohun.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/siroisiinariyamakohun.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/siroisiinariyamakohun.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]]

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/siroisiinariyamakohun.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/siroisiinariyamakohun.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/siroisiinariyamakohun.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gunma Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | native_name =白石稲荷山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 白石稲荷山古墳出土 石枕.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Stone pillow found at Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|16|N|139|02|25|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/siroisiinariyamakohun.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun'''|白石稲荷山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Shiroishi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1993.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209790|title=白石稲荷山古墳|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of several ''kofun'' in the Shiroishi Kofun cluster. The National Historic designation was expanded in the year 2009 to cover two nearby ''kofun'': the {{nihongo|'''Junitenzuka Kofun'''|十二天塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun'''|十二天塚北古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/siroisiinariyamakohun.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}}

==Overview== The Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun tumulus is located on hilly terrain 1.5 km south of the confluence of the Aburagawa and Ayugawa rivers at an elevation of 120 meters, and is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' tumuli in the area. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above with a total length of 155 meters and was built in three tiers, partially by cutting away natural hills to the north and south, and utilizing a natural valley to the east and cliff to the west. The first and second tiers were originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with rows of cylindrical and cone-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' encircled the posterior circular portion of the mound. It is unknown if the tumulus had a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Gunma-Fujioka Station|Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Preliminary investigations were conducted in 1933 and a new [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1985 to 1986. In the 1933 excavation, two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s in the posterior circular portion of the tumulus were found and opened and a number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including two unique two stone pillows. Other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a large number of stone imitations of various everyday items, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron weapons, ''[[magatama]]'' and round beads were excavated. These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A [[:en:Ground penetrating radar|Ground penetrating radar]] survey was performed by the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History and [[Waseda University]] from April 2018 to May 2019. The survey found that the tumulus length, which was thought to be 140 meters, was actually 155 meters, and the tumulus was re-dated to at least the beginning of the 5th century AD. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Gunma-Fujioka Station|Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hachikō Line|Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

These items and also ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. In addition, the presence of a third burial chamber within the anterior rectangular portion of the tumulus was found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun''' *Overall length: 155 meters *Posterior circle: 92 meter diameter x 13.5 meters high *Anterior rectangular portion: 81 meter width x 8.5 meters high
'''Junitenzuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 36.8 meters *Posterior circle: 26.8 meter diameter x 2.6 meters high
'''Junitenkitazuka Kofun''' *Overall length: 23 meters *Posterior circle: 22 meter diameter x 2.2 meters high
The tumuli are located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Gunma-Fujioka Station|Gunma-Fujioka Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hachikō Line|Hachikō Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
